reasoning: Stock prices over short horizons approximate a random walk. For any given starting date, the probability the price will be higher on a future date is roughly 50%, with a very slight upward bias due to general equity risk premiums and positive drift. The instruction specifically mandates that probabilities for more distant dates must be closer to 0.5. Thus, I assign 52% for the nearest date (one week out), 51% for the second date (one month out), and 50% for the third date (three months out).
